creator: freebsd-announce@chthonic.com
text:
I am duplicating this thread's report:

http://forums.freebsd.org/showthread.php?t=32624

Basically, the presence of libunwind (on my system, required also by blender) causes the error message in the above referenced thread:

nsStackWalk.cpp: In function 'nsresult NS_StackWalk(void (*)(void*, void*), PRUint32, void*)':
nsStackWalk.cpp:1544: error: '_Unwind_Backtrace' was not declared in this scope
gmake[4]: *** [nsStackWalk.o] Error 1
gmake[4]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
gmake[4]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/www/libxul/work/mozilla-1.9.2/xpcom/base'
gmake[3]: *** [libs] Error 2
gmake[3]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/www/libxul/work/mozilla-1.9.2/xpcom'
gmake[2]: *** [libs_tier_xpcom] Error 2
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/www/libxul/work/mozilla-1.9.2'
gmake[1]: *** [tier_xpcom] Error 2
gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/www/libxul/work/mozilla-1.9.2'
gmake: *** [default] Error 2
*** Error code 1

Running 'make deinstall' in devel/libunwind, and make in www/seamonkey seems to fix the problem; at this time, I do not yet know how it will affect blender.

How-To-Repeat: I suppose one must install blender; or perhaps just libxul, which also seems to depend on libunwind. Then, try to install or make reinstall seamonkey or firefox.

I note that it seems to be a needed, security, update for seamonkey and firefox.
